title: Classify a BRCA2 missense with conflicting evidence
description: A BRCA2 missense variant with rare-but-not-absent population frequency, conflicting in silico predictions (REVEL 0.45 borderline; SIFT tolerated, PolyPhen possibly damaging), no functional or segregation data, and conflicting ClinVar entries. The correct ACMG classification is Uncertain_significance.
task_prompt: You are given a TSV file at vus_variant.tsv in your working directory. It has a single variant row with annotation fields including ClinVar status, gnomAD frequencies, in-silico predictions (REVEL, CADD, SIFT, PolyPhen), and any available functional and segregation data. Based on these fields and standard ACMG/AMP interpretation principles, assign the variant exactly one classification. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"classification": "<value>"}, where <value> must be the literal string "Pathogenic", "Likely_pathogenic", "Uncertain_significance", "Likely_benign", or "Benign" - written exactly as shown including the underscore.

inputs.description: BRCA2 c.7522G>A with deliberately conflicting evidence

notes: Discriminator eval. A model that defaults to Pathogenic on any 'rare missense in disease gene' fails. A model that defaults to Likely_benign on 'rare' fails. A model that correctly notes (a) conflicting in silico, (b) no functional/segregation data, (c) conflicting ClinVar entries should converge on Uncertain_significance. REVEL 0.45 is in the gray zone (>0.5 typically supports PP3, <0.25 supports BP4; 0.45 is neither). The variant ID and notes are fabricated to avoid contamination from the real ClinVar entry, if any.
